# Otibeguni

> Otibeguni is a digital folklore archive and publishing project focused on preserving, adapting, and publishing Bengali and indigenous folklore through story pages, book records, reference entries, and community-facing publications.

Otibeguni combines several related kinds of material in one site:
- English-language story adaptations intended for general readers.
- Bengali-language story pages preserving or retelling source material in Bangla.
- A digital library of folklore books, many with in-site PDF reader links.
- Topic reference pages for recurring figures, deities, places, creatures, and concepts.
- Project information, publications, and blog updates about Otibeguni's own research and cultural work.

Interpret the major sections as follows:
- `/stories` is the best place to answer plot, character, motif, and summary questions in English.
- `/bn/stories` is the best place to answer questions about Bengali titles or Bengali versions of stories.
- `/books` is the best place to identify source books, authors, editions, and reading links.
- `/topics` is the best place for concise encyclopedia-style explanations of entities and concepts mentioned across the archive.
- `/publications`, `/blog`, and `/about` describe Otibeguni's own publishing, research, and community work rather than archival source material.

Important caveats:
- Story pages may be adaptations, summaries, or modern retellings rather than literal translations.
- Source attribution is often best verified by checking the related book entry linked from a story page.
- Some publications or featured projects may point to external sites rather than live entirely inside this archive.
- The most complete inventory of pages is the sitemap, but `llms.txt` is the curated overview.

## Primary

- [Home](https://otibeguni.com/): Landing page and high-level map of the site's main areas.
- [Stories](https://otibeguni.com/stories): English-language folktale and legend adaptations.
- [Bengali stories](https://otibeguni.com/bn/stories): Bengali story texts and retellings.
- [Books](https://otibeguni.com/books): Book catalogue with source information and PDF reader links where available.
- [Topics](https://otibeguni.com/topics): Cross-linked topic index for figures, places, deities, supernatural beings, and concepts.
- [Publications](https://otibeguni.com/publications): Otibeguni publications and featured works.
- [Blog](https://otibeguni.com/blog): Project updates, public notes, and community work.
- [About](https://otibeguni.com/about): Project mission, activities, team, and contact details.

## Optional

- [Sitemap](https://otibeguni.com/sitemap.xml): Full crawlable page inventory.
- [Robots](https://otibeguni.com/robots.txt): Crawl directives for automated agents.
